title: Angel Motivations Adam Lilith Confusion
wikitext:
[]: Angel Motivations / Adam-Lilith Confusion             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Nov 20, 2008 12:18 pm If third impact couldn't be initiated simply with Lilith, what was the motivation of all angels (before the Adam embryo) for attacking tokyo-3?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 20, 2008 1:58 pm The Angels apparently can't tell the difference between Adam and Lilith, as Kaworu's confusion seems to reply. The Angels basically detect a Seed of Life, and since their instincts can only account for one Seed at a time, they instantly assume it's THEIR mommy.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 20, 2008 3:04 pm they most likely want to know why the world isn't 'right' and what all these little aggressive metal things everywhere are.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Thu Nov 20, 2008 5:01 pm This make me think about sachiel when he is so puzzled by those metal"flys" and what they are trying to do. Kinda remembers me the scene when a fly what to break though a window because it don't reallize that its glas and not air..same with humans here shooting at sachiel but lol at field ,dont you see?*sachiel thought*

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Nov 20, 2008 5:59 pm But lilith was an empty shell for the entire series.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 20, 2008 6:29 pm Some useful stuff in here: http://www.evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=3277 http://www.evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=1914

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Nov 20, 2008 7:09 pm FallenTabris wrote: But lilith was an empty shell for the entire series.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Nov 20, 2008 7:25 pm Motive is what distinguishes between a man killing something, and an animal killing something. Did the Angels really have motives at all? Did they want to intitiate 3rd Impact? Or was it just an instinct to return to their mother, even if they were a bit confused which was which? NemZ wrote: Something did say "Welcome home", remember?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Nov 20, 2008 10:19 pm I think its that before Adam showed up in episode 8, they were attracted to the Evas in Tokyo-3, the clones of Adam, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Nov 20, 2008 10:50 pm Then why did Sachiel attack Shogouki? She was made of SoL material as well, just from Lilith. Wouldn't Sachiel attempt to physically merge with Shogouki instead? Or am i reading this incorrectly?

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Thu Nov 20, 2008 11:10 pm Sachi_13 wrote: Motive is what distinguishes between a man killing something, and an animal killing something. Did the Angels really have motives at all? Did they want to intitiate 3rd Impact? Or was it just an instinct to return to their mother, even if they were a bit confused which was which? NemZ wrote: Something did say "Welcome home", remember?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 21, 2008 12:27 am TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: Then why did Sachiel attack Shogouki? She was made of SoL material as well, just from Lilith. Wouldn't Sachiel attempt to physically merge with Shogouki instead? Or am i reading this incorrectly?

[]:             Quote Post by Ark » Fri Nov 21, 2008 5:00 am Maybe Gaghiel on his way to Tokyo-3 could sense Adam at sea only because she was close by.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Nov 21, 2008 6:23 am V wrote: it attacks anything it perceives as trying to stop it; i.e. that's why it attacks the UN military

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 21, 2008 1:00 pm Like everyone else, the Angels do whatever the script tells them to. Being very young children, however, they easily get duped into appearing dumber and more inconsistent than most of the cast. The ones that are regarded the best tend to have just gotten lucky with the scripts they've landed. When asked why she would let her children accept such plot hole-infested roles, Adam provided no comment.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 21, 2008 1:22 pm UrsusArctos wrote: That was only after getting fried by the N2 mine. Sachiel was remarkably unaggressive before that.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Nov 21, 2008 1:57 pm But lilith was an empty shell for the entire series.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 21, 2008 2:18 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Yea, so? The power of the Seed of Life resides in the body, not the soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Fri Nov 21, 2008 3:14 pm TO the quote the Joker "Motive? I'm like a dog chasing cars, I wouldn't know what to do with one, if a caugth it. I just DO!"

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Nov 21, 2008 3:52 pm Why would Adam be an empty Shell? A giant of light walking around and provoking a second impact isn't what I would consider an empty Shell. After that he just became an embryo, and since an embryo doesn't move much... I wouldn't compare it to Lilith who's only action had been to bleed around to give birth.
summary:
[]: Angel Motivations / Adam-Lilith Confusion             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Nov 20, 2008 12:18 pm If third impact couldn't be initiated simply with Lilith, what was the motivation of all angels (before the Adam embryo) for attacking tokyo-3?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 20, 2008 1:58 pm The Angels apparently can't tell the differe
